reviews.review_title: Good game world, frustratingly easy game
reviews.review_description: This game is charming, and the world and characters are well done. It is a little like the new x-coms, or Shadowrun. But it is very easy, with no ability to scale the difficulty. I never died once, and none of the combat ever felt challenging. There is a skill unlock progression but rarely ever any incentive to use any of the higher leveled skills. As far as I can tell, grenades are totally useless. There is so much collectable loot that there is never any need to buy items in the shop, so I ended the game with a full inventory and all of the money collected throughout unspent. I am still giving it four stars since the interface is fun and the content absorbing (so long as you treat it like a movie and not a game).

reviews.review_title: Amazing game with a stellar voice cast.
reviews.review_description:
A hidden VR gem most users haven't heard about. The Narrator from Baldur's Gate 3 is one of the first characters you hear in this game, and one of the main characters is Jennifer English herself, aka Shadowheart & Maelle. A number of other VA's in the game are notable as well, including the voice of Geralt of Rivia from The Witcher. A number of them were also smaller roles in Divinity Original Sin 2, which was where Jennifer English had her first video game role.

The game itself has a great story, atmosphere, and music. It was also one of the few cross-buy games between older Oculus headsets and the new Meta ones when it got a full update. Simple enough gameplay to get into easily as well.

You are looking for long gameplay? Buy this! Amazing story, longest gameplay I have encountered on vr. 

You made an amazing job with this one! 

I would love be to zoom just a little bit more on to the table, on gear VR are small details too blurry. It would be amazing if you could implement the option to zoom a little bit closer into scenario.
A fun game with a good story. A bit simple in combat but creative overall. I haven't been to the end yet but am enjoying it.

This is by far my favorite Go or Quest VR game. I wish it used the Go controller. This game is so awesome I gave up on the Quest until I finish this game on my Go.  I theory the quest will eventually play all Go games including this one but not yet. 
I loved the characters and the voice acting. I found the combat challenging but doable. The story was definitely involved. 
Overall really awesome.

One star deducted because, as others have said, you will get neck ache due to lack of Go control in places. Other than that this is a really fun, immersive RPG which works well in VR. One I keep coming back to again and again. Yes it could be longer but it is still 5 plus hrs.

Such a creative a fun game an alright story but let's be honest none of us were looking for triple a level story telling. Great graphics with easy to use controls would recomend this as introduction to this sort of play style.

reviews.review_title: Great game with only one flaw.
reviews.review_description: It would be 5 stars from me but there is no difficulty option and the game is very easy - i never died or had to heal. Would love to see an update with difficulty scaling with current state as Easy.

I notice on being very critical when it comes to games. VR or regular. Perhaps I am spoiled from playing a lot of great games through the years, maybe it's because I am a nitpicker. My wife will agree with the latter.

Management summary: This game is a narrative game with a turn-based combat added with a small portion of tactics in between scenes. I find this game is truly classy allround. If I look through my whole GO library this is probably the best game overall. And it is the only narrative/game I have completed so far. And that is saying something.

I'll save you a long story and summarize what I found bout this game:

+ production - the fields are well designed and beautiful, very much in sync with the theme. The fade in's and out's when you start the game/narrative, the slight tilting of the board, and the VR experience they have provided on the tabletop or your planning/master control room is very elegant, polished. There is a lot more to production value that has been well thought through. It breathes labour of love.

+ graphics - Beautiful design of characters, nice, functional framerate, and marvelous use of colors in the scenery. Nice effects. If I had it my way I would have loved for the graphics to be a tad sharper. But it was no real nuisance during playing

+ interface - Really, really nice. Nice use of controller or head movement. Well implemented controls a-la-XCOM, with an easy submenu to call on the extra powers and functionalities of the personas in this game.

+ Storyline: absolutely wonderful. At first I was just aiming to get to play a turn-based tactical game, but the story is so well designed that I found myself getting "into" the story. It seems that the decisions and advice you provide influence the storyline. Some interactivity it seems. It really felt like I played in a movie. Some nice plot and game-twists near the end. Very surprising, a bit titillating even.
Any minuses? Not enough to mention

Truly and honestly recommended.

It is nice but gets repetitive fast. Super long - like 15 hours - 

A lot of voice acting.  And i mean too much.  Blah blah blah. The story is ok but too long.  When i killed Bugsy, i thought the game was done but nope.  blah blah.

The actual fighting is the core of the game.  It is ok but super easy. All the Augments and stuff of team management are pretty useless since you end up pretty much always shooting.

The highlight of the game is when Willa actually enters the office. That was great
I love this game! Lots of voice acting, some cool abilities, great graphics. I am really enjoying this game.
So im writing review as i download this and will update as i download, install, and hopefully play.  3 stars for now for all the good reviews
Amazing to watch...
I was on the fence about getting this game and thank God I did.  Wonderful game and wonderful story.
The best VR game I've played yet. It needs full Oculus controller support though. I hate having to move my head in awkward directions to select stuff.
This game isn't immersive at all, may as well be playing on a PC. Not really what I have a vr console for.

Just finished.  As far as Oculus Go games,  this is by far the longest play of any title I've purchased.  The gameplay is well done and the story is good.  Lots of characters with different abilities.  Well worth the money I spent.

Man. I have to say this is the first VR game I get excited to play (as cliche as that probably sounds at this point). I love RPG games, so was disappointed in the lack of RPG games for the Go ( still thinking of getting Quest soon ) but this game really does the RPG Genre well. I love the turn-based combat, it has a intriguing story line, and the voice acting is phenomenal. I love hearing Willa talk.

reviews.review_title: Good game but way too easy
reviews.review_description: This is a fun tactics game but without an adjustable difficulty setting the game is extremely easy. At least include a hard mode for people who like a challenge in tactics games please.

I bought this game amongst a dozen other games when I got my Oculus Go. Originally, i tried it but didnt get into it. I came back to it 3-4 days ago and let me tell you. I got the hang out it, got hooked, and just finished the game.
FIRST THE GOOD. The story line is awesome, you feel like you are playing a movie, the voices, the scenes, the plot,  all well directed (nice soundtrack too). The game is nice and long (at least 7-8 hrs of gameplay) with a bunch of different characters that you all get on your team. Various "augment" and "overload" choices and selectable characters per mission, even various dialogue choices; all give it a custom feel that you're choices impact the game. 
This game is well thoight out, well executed, and only cost a fraction of a console game. Stop spending $60 on Xbox or PS games and try this one. I spent my own money and feel like i got my monies worth and this has to stay in the collection.
Many times, after a few missions, ill get up to get a drink a literally say out loud "man this is a great game". That compelled me to write this review.


GRIPES, if any. During combat the namebar of an enemy or player can block your view.

THOUGHTS, the looking-down-at-a-room view works well and is comfortable. Also, the look-to-aim works fine and isnt bothersome UNTIL you get back in the apartment and have to break your neck over your left shoulder to use the phone. During combat LTA works fine, you aren't pressed for time, smooth head motions. DEVS, please just give the option to use Go controller to aim as secondary. 

Thank you for a great game and I hope there is a part 2 coming.

developer_response.body: Hey InsertCashForFreedom, thank you so much for your review. We're glad to hear that you had a great time playing Augmented Empire.
